---
title: Conceptual Models of the HEMS System
---

## Initial Version

![](assets/2024-11-05-10-16-14.png)

![](assets/2024-11-05-10-16-28.png)

## Version 2

![](assets/DAAT%20Model%20Flowchart.png)





:::{.callout-note}
Questions arising (SR)

## Operational questions

- Is current presence of doctor on only some calls due to the design of the older helicopter?

## Servicing questions

- How do servicing intervals/lengths vary across the different resources?
- How often does servicing extend beyond the planned timing, and by how much? Does this vary by helicopter type?

## Helicopter questions

- What is the maximum expected remaining operational life on that helicopter?
- What are the likely servicing costs of keeping the 135 running over the same period vs a new helicopter



:::


Richard Applegarth - pilot - been pilot for long time, specifically with DAAT for 3 years

Paul White - paramedic, APP (advanced) , more car work than general paramedic cohort, been around the ambulance service for nearly 20 years

Lee - critical care paramedic, APP, car shifts, ambulance service for a number of years

Richard P - Paramedic from Yorkshire Ambulance Service, research data analysts
Paramedic for 25 years

Lauren - HEMS dispatchers/dispatch team years (on 5 years)

Nigel - Critical care paramedics, DAAT for 15 years, also has a research paramedic job

Josh - APP with DAAT, 5 years, about to embark on a control job, middle of night shifts (lates)


APP -



MAGPAS, EPAS and
They did the work on golden codes with the high incidence of HEMS

There's always been a big question around whether the demand is being met around the area, and they've

Lee H raises question around the SWAST data
- have been positive initial meetings around 'yes, we can support this', but nothing further since
- Hannah unsure whether we will get it before xmas (Hannah's initial deadline for a working model)
    - Will be needing to lean on Darren to push SWAST
- Question around 'can we plug this in later if we get it later?'
    - Hannah assures yes
- Hannah assures that we can validate against currently held data
    - *TODO: how can we build in automated validation scripts to plan ahead the
- This is the 'contingency model' being built on our own data

- Hannah communicates to them the split of the model phases and what the DES element is focussing on doing
(I think there is also just a split of approach of how the DES is sampling times/journeys here)

What does the presence of DAAT do for the number of land crews?

Contingency model will be looking at CAD category in HEMSbase
- Hannah Q for clinicians - how accurate do they feel this is?
    - it's going to determine various parameter
    - Job type on job page?
    - A lot of different options that they have to scroll down, does the language fit with what they are attending (generally done retrospectively)
        - There is some uncertainty
        - Not passed to them by 'Airbox'? mentioned being passed on the car?
    - Expecting
    - Richard pointing out that this is almost 'retrospectively super-accurate' (even if it sometimes isn't filled)
        - Richard P says feeling is that we as modelling team (and DAAT more generally) really need the SWAST data!
    - Richard P raises that for the model: we need to decide what matters
    - Comes into the potential for generalisability
        - East of England paper
    - Example given of call coming in as a head injury - but actually on site it's a fall that's then developed into a cardiac arrest. How do you categorise?
    - Richard highlighting that we will need to be super clear on what
    - They get this code on the car but not the aircraft - questions
    - Richard P raises the separation of the clinical need/value here vs the audit value

- Discussions around how the model is limited by this lack of data
    - Richard separating out what
    - And what impact are you going to have on making changes to internal services - can
    - *thought - can we at least answer some questions about 'what if actual demand is ~ 10%, 20%, 30% higher?*
        - *how does this reflect the *
        -

- Paul White raising the
    - Richard P saying about how we can have an estimate/adjustment in the model where a small proportion that isn't golden code/adjustment
    - So additional ways that we are adjusting this proportion

- Lee Hilton also mentioning the possibility of HEMS requests coming in from
    - Richard P raising - how important is this going to be from the model's perspective
    -

- Nigel Lang mentioning
    - crew despatched to incident, mobile, then put a request before they get to the scene for HEMS
    - often then they are told 'not until you're there - give us an update'
        - unsure how often this is happening
    - Lauren H senses this isn't hugely frequent
        - a 'cry wolf' element
        - Suggest there is frequently the

- Lee H mentioning there is a North geographical bias
    - *can HT expand on what this means?*

- Lee H responding
    - "Yes, sometimes. Or because of condition worsening. HEMS won’t allocate unless there is good suggestion of immediate need but worsening pain does fall into this category (so a broad number of patients fall within this category)"


- Nigel L mentioning additional ways in which the crew can add value even if it's outside of
    - e.g. an unwitnessed cardiac arrest
    - (mentioning the helicopter vs the cars in this case - )
    - Richard P mentioning how hard some of these benefits are to quantify
        - *SR thought: coming at it from the DES side - looking at currently *

- *TODO: is there some sort of clustering we can do here?*

- *TODO: Question from me - for inter-hospital transfers, are these arranged in a different way?*

-
    - Lee H: "Agree with Paul W - they should definitely include the softer skills and we know this brings the most benefit (was reflected in the VRS report by a significant margin over procedural skills)"
    - *SR thought: is there some kind of NLP we could do to pull some of this out? Sounds like they maybe have some sort of labelled dataset - or just
    - Richard P does mention that we need
        - needing to point out that
        - this model is decision support

- Lee H mentioning the possibility of

- Josh B: says can't help retrospectively, but suggesting that could be additional categories added like 'advanced decision making'
    - Richard P says 'is there ever a time this doesn't happen?'
    - Josh B says there will be times this isn't the case
        - intending that this will be closer
    - Paul W - adding in things like 'debriefing' as well
        - sense tat they know
    - *SR thoughts: can we just get expert estimates at this point to incorporate this in some way? How much does this impact the model versus not?*


Terminology
- ANPDS code?
- NMA?
- VRS Report
